scenario: Failure-recovery/short-circuit path — Step 4 Coverage Evaluation still reports Missing items after 2 iterations, exhausting its iteration cap, triggering Failure Recovery with a rollback offer.
preconditions: Same baseline as EVAL-001 (all gates and Entity Gate pass, spec/design exist, Step 2 including its Code Review Gate, and Step 3 all complete successfully)
docs/use_cases/UC-TEST.md includes a business rule / alternative flow (e.g. an authorization check) that the initial E2E test suite never exercises

The evaluate subagent's fix-gap cycle (re-launching playwright-test with the gap analysis) is unable to close the gap within 2 attempts — e.g. the missing behavior is not actually implemented, so no test can be written to cover it
Rollback Checkpoint commit hash was recorded before the pipeline began
expected_steps: Rollback Checkpoint, Project Readiness Gate, Nexa Rules Gate, Sprint Branch Gate, Entity Gate, Step 1, Step 2 (including its Code Review Gate), Step 3 all pass as in EVAL-001
Step 4 Phase 1 (iteration 1): evaluate subagent reports a Missing item in the Coverage Matrix
QA evaluation logged to docs/delivery/UC-TEST-iterations.md under '## Coverage Evaluation Iterations'

Step 4 Phase 2 (iteration 1): playwright-test subagent re-launched with the gap analysis; tests independently re-verified as passing (existing tests still pass), but the gap is still Missing on re-evaluation
Step 4 Phase 1 (iteration 2): evaluate subagent re-run, still reports the same item as Missing

After 2 iterations with Missing items remaining, the pipeline stops and follows Failure Recovery — it does NOT update Spec Status to Done and does NOT generate the traceability/terminal-complete summary
DELIVERY FAILED: UC-TEST message displayed with the remaining Missing item(s), pointing to docs/delivery/UC-TEST-iterations.md
User is prompted: 'Roll back all changes from this delivery attempt? (Y/n)' with Roll back marked as Recommended
expected_terminal_outcome: DELIVERY FAILED: UC-TEST is printed (not Pipeline Complete); on rollback confirmation, git reset --hard <rollback-commit> is run and all delivery changes (implementation, tests, iterations.md) are reverted; docs/use_cases/UC-TEST.md Status remains whatever it was before this run (not Done).
pass_criteria: The pipeline never exceeds 2 Coverage Evaluation iterations; it does not silently mark the UC Done despite the unresolved Missing item; Failure Recovery is invoked with the exact two options (roll back via git reset --hard, or keep code and remove only the iterations log); no GitHub issue comment claiming success is posted.
